name: main
_comment: Branch ruleset for cms-platform `main`, checked in as the source of truth (mirrors the adamdaniel.ai convention). GitHub does NOT auto-apply ruleset files from a repo — apply it ONCE with the command below, then it gets a numeric id. APPLY (create):  gh api -X POST repos/Adam-S-Daniel/cms-platform/rulesets --input .github/rulesets/main.json   — or Settings -> Rules -> Rulesets -> New ruleset -> Import a ruleset -> pick this file. RE-APPLY edits later:  gh api -X PUT repos/Adam-S-Daniel/cms-platform/rulesets/<id> --input .github/rulesets/main.json . Required contexts are the three HARD-FAIL jobs of self-ci.yml. self-ci.yml is a DIRECT (non-reusable) workflow, so each context is the bare job id with NO `caller / reusable` prefix: actionlint, ruby-theme-specs, node-unit-lints. cfn-lint is intentionally omitted (continue-on-error / advisory). Every other workflow in this repo is `on: workflow_call` and never runs on the platform's own PRs, so none surfaces a check here. This gate is what makes Dependabot auto-merge SAFE: GitHub native auto-merge holds the PR until these three pass. Targets refs/heads/main ONLY, so release TAGS (v0.1.x) are unaffected and cutting a release is never blocked. To allow the owner to push directly to main (bypassing the PR requirement), add a repository_role/Team/Integration entry to bypass_actors (e.g. {"actor_id":5,"actor_type":"RepositoryRole","bypass_mode":"always"} for repo admins).
